headlines.title: Japan government approves $3.2B yen use from reserve fund for energy bill subsidy
ECB eyes June rate hike as chief economist flags forecast upgrade
Japan stocks lower at close of trade; Nikkei 225 down 0.29%
Global Markets: Japan's Nikkei retreats from record high on profit-booking, strength in oil prices
Japan shares lower at close of trade; Nikkei 225 down 0.29%
Japan's Nikkei retreats from record high on profit-booking, strength in oil prices
Japan's Nikkei retreats from record high on profit-booking, strength in oil prices

marketTone.ko: 오늘 일본 증시는 차익 실현 매물 출회와 유가 강세의 영향으로 하락 마감했습니다. 닛케이 225 지수는 최근 기록적인 고점에서 0.29% 하락하며 조정을 받았습니다. 투자자들은 사상 최고치 경신 이후 차익 실현에 나섰으며, 유가 상승 또한 시장에 부담으로 작용했습니다. 한편, 일본 정부는 에너지 요금 보조금으로 32억 달러 규모의 예비비를 사용하기로 승인했습니다.
marketTone.en: The Japanese stock market closed lower today, with the Nikkei 225 retreating 0.29% from its recent record highs. This downturn was primarily attributed to profit-booking activities following the market's strong performance. Additionally, strength in oil prices was noted as a contributing factor. While the government approved a $3.2 billion yen energy subsidy, the overall market sentiment was dominated by the retreat and profit-taking pressures.
